confidence_rationale: Clear view of knot formation sequence and hand switching pattern. Camera angle limits precise assessment of suture placement relative to marks. No clear penalties observed from available frames.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation from marks - camera angle and resolution limit precise measurement
Whether suture ends were cut inside trainer - final frames do not show cutting phase clearly
technique_summary: Competent intermediate performance with proper knot sequence (surgeon's knot followed by two single throws) and correct alternating hand technique. Time of 150 seconds places in intermediate range.
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot formation with double throw
Consistent alternating hand pattern throughout all throws
Clean knot formation with appropriate tension
No visible drain damage or knot failure
improvement_suggestions: Practice to reduce overall time from 150s toward expert range (80-120s)
Focus on efficiency during throw formation to minimize time loss
